subject: [PATCH v2 07/28] fsnotify: add FSNOTIFY_EVENT_RENAME data type

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary: Reviewer noted that NFS protocol requires reporting the overwritten node in the same event as the rename, and expressed acceptance of this requirement.
reviews.raw_body:
It is strange to me that the NFS protocol needs to report the overwritten
node in the same event of the rename, but oh well, fine by me.

Feel free to add:
Reviewed-by: Amir Goldstein <amir73il@gmail.com>

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: The author acknowledges that the new FSNOTIFY_EVENT_RENAME data type is not very useful but justifies its existence due to protocol requirements and historical context.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, it's not very useful, but the protocol requires it. Unfortunately
RFC5661 was written before anyone had made a real implementation of
directory delegations. If we were rewriting it today, we'd probably
make that info optional.

2026-04-16.patch_summary: This patch adds a new data type FSNOTIFY_EVENT_RENAME to the fsnotify system, which allows consumers like nfsd to atomically observe the overwritten file when a rename operation replaces an existing entry. The new data type carries both the moved dentry and the inode that was overwritten by the rename. The patch updates various functions in the fsnotify system to handle this new data type, including fsnotify_move(), fsnotify_data_inode(), and fsnotify_data_dentry(). This change enables more efficient handling of rename operations and improves the accuracy of notifications for file system events.
